name: Empty Mountains - circular through the Puebla de San Miguel Natural Park
name_original: Montañas Vacías – circular por el Parque Natural de la Puebla de San Miguel
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Riodeva traverse a landscape characterized by varied natural environments and geological formations in the province of Teruel. The region features the Vía Verde de Ojos Negros, a former railway line offering a generally downhill trajectory towards the coast. Mountainous terrain defines areas like Los Amanaderos, where the Riodeva River carves waterfalls and natural pools. The Puebla de San Miguel Natural Park, visible from Riodeva, presents geological elements from past mining activities. Best touring cycling routes around Riodeva The most popular touring cycling route is Empty Mountains - circular by Javalambre, a 20.5 miles (33.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 39 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the rugged, natural environments of the Javalambre area. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Empty Mountains - circular through the Puebla de San Miguel Natural Park, a difficult 25.0 miles (40.3 km) path. This route offers insights into the geological elements of the natural park. Local touring cyclists also love the The Amanaderos of Riodeva – Fuente de Matahombres loop from Riodeva, a 20.9 miles (33.6 km) trail leading through the Los Amanaderos area, often completed in about 3 hours 47 minutes. This route passes by the characteristic waterfalls and river formations. Touring cycling around Riodeva is defined by greenways like the Vía Verde de Ojos Negros, mountainous terrain with waterfalls at Los Amanaderos, and geological features within the Puebla de San Miguel Natural Park. The network offers options for experienced cyclists seeking challenging routes. The routes in Riodeva are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 3 reviews. More than 50 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Riodeva's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Riodeva?
A: There are 6 touring cycling routes available around Riodeva, all of which are considered difficult, offering a challenge for experienced cyclists.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Riodeva?
A: The best times to undertake touring cycling routes in Riodeva are typically spring and autumn. This helps avoid the extreme temperatures that can occur during summer and winter months.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Riodeva?
A: While the routes directly around Riodeva are rated as difficult, the nearby Vía Verde de Ojos Negros is particularly well-suited for family cycling tours. Its generally downhill trajectory when cycled from the interior towards the coast makes it accessible for various fitness levels.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in the Riodeva area?
A: Yes, several circular touring cycling routes are available. For example, you can explore the rugged environments with the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e955771784/empty-mountains-circular-by-javalambre" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Empty Mountains - circular by Javalambre&lt;/a&gt;, or discover geological features on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e956347125/empty-mountains-circular-through-the-puebla-de-san-miguel-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Empty Mountains - circular through the Puebla de San Miguel Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any routes that feature waterfalls or natural pools?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31502176" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Amanaderos of Riodeva – Fuente de Matahombres loop from Riodeva&lt;/a&gt; leads through the Los Amanaderos area, which is celebrated for its series of waterfalls and natural pools formed by the Riodeva River. This area is perfect for enjoying the natural beauty, especially during summer.

Q: What kind of natural landscapes can I expect to see while cycling around Riodeva?
A: Touring cycling around Riodeva offers diverse natural environments. You'll encounter mountainous terrain, particularly in areas like Los Amanaderos with its dramatic waterfalls, and geological formations within the Puebla de San Miguel Natural Park. The region also features the Vía Verde de Ojos Negros, which traverses varied landscapes from rugged natural settings to old towns and villages.

Q: What are some interesting places to explore or landmarks near the cycling routes?
A: Beyond the cycling routes, Riodeva offers unique attractions. You can visit &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4457070" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Amanaderos of Riodeva&lt;/a&gt; for its stunning waterfalls, or explore the paleontological site of Titania (Dinópolis), which features a life-size replica of a Turiasaurus skeleton. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3925573" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Javalambre&lt;/a&gt; is also a notable landmark in the wider area.

Q: Is the Vía Verde de Ojos Negros accessible from Riodeva?
A: Riodeva is in close proximity to the Vía Verde de Ojos Negros, Spain's longest greenway. This former mining railway line offers an excellent touring cycling experience, connecting the interior of Teruel with the Valencian coast. You can find more information about this route on &lt;a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/V%C3%ADa_Verde_de_Ojos_Negros" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Wikipedia&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Riodeva?
A: The touring cycling routes in Riodeva are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 3 reviews. Cyclists often praise the challenging nature of the routes and the immersive experience in the region's rugged, natural environments.

Q: Are there any routes that offer insights into the region's geology or mining history?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e956347125/empty-mountains-circular-through-the-puebla-de-san-miguel-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Empty Mountains - circular through the Puebla de San Miguel Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; offers insights into the rich geological elements exposed by past mining activities, with informative panels along a geological route at the base of the 'Peña del Águila' peak.

Q: What is the longest touring cycling route available directly from Riodeva?
A: One of the longer and more challenging routes is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38215809" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Javalambre – Viaducto de Peñaflores loop from Camarena de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt;, which covers approximately 91.3 kilometers (56.7 miles) with significant elevation changes.

Impressive juniper forest where the majority of specimens are over 400 years old, one of them reaching almost a thousand years of age.
This refugio at 1,720 metres altitude on the Sierra de Javalambre is basic, but the roof is completely new and the interior has been restored. It has a special charm.

Its name refers to the surge from where the Riodeva is born. Not only is the landscape and environment of the Amanaderos something unique, but so is the path to get here. From the town of Riodeva, follow a path that in sections coincides with the GR 10 and runs along the river ravine between steps, boulevards and footbridges. A route that also runs through several waterfalls depending on the flow of the river depending on the rains.
